prompt: A formula is a CONTINGENCY when:
options: It is true in every row of its truth table
It is false in every row of its truth table
It is true in some rows and false in others
hint: Contingent = it depends. Its truth varies with the values of its variables.
explanation: A contingency is true in some rows and false in others — its truth value depends on the assignment (most ordinary statements are contingent). 'True in every row' is a tautology; 'false in every row' is a contradiction. Together, tautology / contradiction / contingency classify every formula.
